battle of lexington and concord.name: Battle of Lexington and Concord
battle of lexington and concord.location: Middlesex County, Province of Massachusetts Bay
battle of lexington and concord.description: The opening engagements of the American Revolutionary War, involving skirmishes between British regulars and colonial militia. The British sought to seize military supplies and capture rebel leaders, resulting in a strategic American victory.
battle of lexington and concord.significance: Marked the beginning of open armed conflict between Great Britain and its thirteen colonies in North America.

markers.label: Lexington Common
markers.description: Site of the 'shot heard 'round the world' and the first confrontation.

markers.label: Pitcairn's Advance
markers.description: Major Pitcairn leads the British vanguard onto the green.

markers.label: Old North Bridge
markers.description: The site where colonial militia first fired on British regulars on orders.

markers.label: Meriam's Corner
markers.description: Beginning of the running skirmish during the British retreat.

markers.label: Percy's Relief Column
markers.description: Lord Percy arrives with reinforcements to save the retreating British troops.

movements.label: March to Lexington
movements.description: British regulars march from Boston under the cover of night.

movements.label: Militia Rally
movements.description: Minutemen from surrounding towns converge on Concord.

movements.label: The Retreat to Boston
movements.description: British forces harassed by constant sniper fire during their return march.

zones.label: Concord Search Area
zones.description: Area where British troops searched for hidden munitions.

zones.label: The Battle Road
zones.description: The primary corridor of the afternoon engagement.

battle of bunker hill.name: Battle of Bunker Hill
battle of bunker hill.description: A bloody assault by British forces against fortified American positions on Breed's Hill. Although the British took the ground, they suffered massive casualties against a determined defense.
battle of bunker hill.significance: Demonstrated that inexperienced militia could stand up to regular British troops in a pitched battle.

markers.label: Prescott's Redoubt
markers.description: The primary earthen fortification built overnight on Breed's Hill.

markers.label: The Rail Fence
markers.description: John Stark's men defend the American left flank.

markers.label: Howe's Landing
markers.description: British troops land at Moulton's Point for the assault.

markers.label: Death of Warren
markers.description: Dr. Joseph Warren is killed during the final British breach.

markers.label: Hill Captured
markers.description: British forces occupy the heights after American ammunition runs out.

movements.label: First British Assault
movements.description: Frontal attack met with devastating fire from the redoubt.

movements.label: Final Bayonet Charge
movements.description: Third and successful British assault after American fire slackens.

movements.label: Retreat to Cambridge
movements.description: American forces withdraw across Charlestown Neck.

zones.label: Breed's Hill Redoubt
zones.description: The main area of American resistance.

zones.label: Burning Charlestown
zones.description: Town set ablaze by British naval shells to dislodge snipers.

battle of long island.name: Battle of Long Island
battle of long island.description: The first major battle after the Declaration of Independence and the largest of the war. British forces outflanked Washington's army, nearly capturing them before a daring nighttime escape.
battle of long island.significance: Gave Britain control of New York City and forced the Continental Army into a long retreat.

markers.label: Heights of Guan
markers.description: Natural defensive ridge held by American forces.

markers.label: Jamaica Pass
markers.description: Unguarded pass used by the British to flank the American line.

markers.label: Maryland 400 Stand
markers.description: Heroic rear-guard action to allow others to escape to Brooklyn Heights.

markers.label: Ferry Landing
markers.description: Washington's army evacuates across the East River in fog.

markers.label: Howe's HQ
markers.description: British headquarters during the initial landing on Staten Island.

movements.label: Clinton's Flanking Maneuver
movements.description: Night march through Jamaica Pass to get behind American lines.

movements.label: Retreat to Fort Greene
movements.description: Scattered American units fall back to the primary fortifications.

movements.label: East River Evacuation
movements.description: Continental Army crosses to Manhattan under cover of darkness.

zones.label: Brooklyn Heights Defenses
zones.description: The final American defensive perimeter.

zones.label: Flatbush Skirmish
zones.description: Hessian troops engage American defenders.

battle of harlem heights.name: Battle of Harlem Heights
battle of harlem heights.description: A morale-boosting engagement where American troops successfully counter-attacked and pushed back British light infantry on Manhattan.
battle of harlem heights.significance: Restored American confidence after the disastrous defeat on Long Island.

markers.label: Knowlton's Rangers
markers.description: American scouting party that initiated the skirmish.

markers.label: The Hollow Way
markers.description: Valley where the main fighting occurred.

markers.label: Buckwheat Field
markers.description: Site of the heaviest combat and British retreat.

markers.label: British Fallback
markers.description: British light infantry retreats to their main lines.

markers.label: Morris-Jumel Mansion
markers.description: Washington's headquarters overlooking the battlefield.

movements.label: American Reconnaissance
movements.description: Rangers move south to find British positions.

movements.label: British Advance
movements.description: British light infantry aggressively pursues the American scouts.

movements.label: Counter-attack
movements.description: Washington sends reinforcements to trap the overextended British.

zones.label: Harlem Heights Perimeter
zones.description: Fortified American positions on northern Manhattan.

zones.label: Primary Engagement Zone
zones.description: The area of the buckwheat field and orchard combat.

battle of white plains.name: Battle of White Plains
battle of white plains.description: Following the retreat from Manhattan, Washington established a defensive position in White Plains. British and Hessian forces drove the Americans from a key hill, but Washington was able to withdraw safely.
battle of white plains.significance: Continued the British push to clear the rebels from the New York area.

markers.label: Chatterton Hill
markers.description: Key high ground on the American right flank.

markers.label: Hessian Charge
markers.description: Col. Rahl's Hessians storm the hill.

markers.label: Main American Line
markers.description: Washington's central defensive positions.

markers.label: British Battery
markers.description: Guns bombarding the American positions on the hill.

markers.label: Withdrawal Point
markers.description: Washington pulls back to North Castle.

movements.label: British Advance
movements.description: Howe's columns move toward the American defenses.

movements.label: Assault on Chatterton Hill
movements.description: British and Hessian troops cross the Bronx River to attack.

movements.label: Orderly Withdrawal
movements.description: Washington retreats under cover of rain and darkness.

zones.label: Chatterton Hill Battlefield
zones.description: The site of the battle's most intense combat.

zones.label: White Plains Encampment
zones.description: Main Continental Army camp.

battle of fort washington.name: Battle of Fort Washington
battle of fort washington.description: A devastating defeat for the Americans where a large garrison was surrounded and forced to surrender to British and Hessian forces.
battle of fort washington.significance: Resulted in the loss of nearly 3,000 American troops and vast military stores.

markers.label: Fort Washington
markers.description: The primary American fortification on Manhattan's highest point.

markers.label: Hessian North Attack
markers.description: Knyphausen leads the main assault from the north.

markers.label: Laurel Hill
markers.description: Site of secondary British attack and American fallback.

markers.label: Surrender Site
markers.description: Magaw surrenders the fort to the British.

markers.label: Jeffrey's Hook
markers.description: Location of American redoubts guarding the river.

movements.label: Hessian Assault
movements.description: Difficult climb through steep terrain and woods.

movements.label: Harlem River Crossing
movements.description: British light infantry lands to attack from the east.

movements.label: Retreat to the Fort
movements.description: Outer defenses fail, forcing troops into the crowded fort.

zones.label: Fort Core
zones.description: The central pentagonal earthen fort.

zones.label: Fort Defenses
zones.description: The wider area of American batteries and redoubts.

battle of trenton.name: Battle of Trenton
battle of trenton.description: After crossing the icy Delaware River, Washington led a surprise dawn attack against a Hessian garrison. The nearly bloodless victory provided a vital boost to American morale.
battle of trenton.significance: Revitalized the American cause during its darkest hour.

markers.label: Crossing Point
markers.description: Site where the army crossed the Delaware River.

markers.label: Knox's Artillery
markers.description: American guns commanded the streets from this high ground.

markers.label: Rall's Headquarters
markers.description: Where the Hessian commander was mortally wounded.

markers.label: Surrender Field
markers.description: Where the Hessian regiments finally laid down their arms.

markers.label: Hessian Picket
markers.description: Outpost that first spotted the American advance.

movements.label: Delaware Crossing
movements.description: Crossing the river amidst floating ice blocks.

movements.label: The Morning March
movements.description: Nine-mile march in a sleet and snow storm.

movements.label: Hessian Retreat
movements.description: Attempt to escape toward Princeton cut off by Americans.

zones.label: Trenton Village
zones.description: The town layout where the street fighting occurred.

zones.label: Old Barracks
zones.description: Housing for the Hessian garrison.

battle of princeton.name: Battle of Princeton
battle of princeton.description: After slipping away from Cornwallis at Trenton, Washington attacked the British rear guard at Princeton, securing another victory before winter quarters.
battle of princeton.significance: Completed the recovery of most of New Jersey and forced British withdrawal to New York.

markers.label: Mercer's Orchard
markers.description: Initial clash where General Mercer was mortally wounded.

markers.label: Washington's Charge
markers.description: Washington personally leads a charge to rally retreating troops.

markers.label: Nassau Hall
markers.description: British troops take refuge here before surrendering.

markers.label: Thomas Clark House
markers.description: Field hospital where General Mercer died.

markers.label: Stony Brook Bridge
markers.description: Passage for both armies' movements.

movements.label: The Silent March
movements.description: Washington's army slips away from Trenton by night.

movements.label: British Counter-march
movements.description: Mawhood turns back to engage the Americans.

movements.label: Flight to New Brunswick
movements.description: Remaining British forces flee toward their main base.

zones.label: Princeton Battlefield
zones.description: The open fields of the main engagement.

zones.label: College Barricade
zones.description: British soldiers fortified inside Nassau Hall.

battle of brandywine.name: Battle of Brandywine
battle of brandywine.description: Howe successfully outflanked Washington's army at Brandywine Creek, leading to a British victory and the subsequent capture of Philadelphia.
battle of brandywine.significance: Cleared the way for the British occupation of the American capital.

markers.label: Chadds Ford
markers.description: Primary crossing point guarded by Wayne's division.

markers.label: Birmingham Meeting House
markers.description: Site of the main engagement after the British flank.

markers.label: Lafayette's Baptism of Fire
markers.description: The young Marquis is wounded while rallying troops.

markers.label: Hessian Diversion
markers.description: Knyphausen's forces feign an attack at the ford.

markers.label: American Fallback
markers.description: The army retreats toward Chester under Greene's cover.

movements.label: Cornwallis's Long March
movements.description: 17-mile march to cross the creek unguarded.

movements.label: Assault at Chadds Ford
movements.description: Frontal attack once the flanking move was heard.

movements.label: Greene's Fighting Retreat
movements.description: Holding off the British to save the army from destruction.

zones.label: Birmingham Hill Battlefield
zones.description: The high ground where Americans tried to reform their line.

zones.label: Chadds Ford Perimeter
zones.description: Earthen works and artillery covering the ford.

battle of paoli.name: Battle of Paoli
battle of paoli.description: Often called the 'Paoli Massacre', this was a night attack by British troops using bayonets to surprise and rout General Anthony Wayne's division.
battle of paoli.significance: A brutal defeat for Wayne's command, resulting in significant American losses and a 'No Quarter' reputation for the British unit involved.

markers.label: Wayne's Encampment
markers.description: The wooded area where American troops were resting.

markers.label: No Flint Grey
markers.description: General Grey orders flints removed to ensure a silent bayonet attack.

markers.label: Main Attack Point
markers.description: British light infantry bursts into the American camp.

markers.label: Retreat to White Horse
markers.description: Survivors flee through the darkness toward Lancaster Road.

markers.label: Admiral Warren Tavern
markers.description: Local landmark near the American position.

movements.label: Night Approach
movements.description: Silent approach guided by local loyalists.

movements.label: The Bayonet Charge
movements.description: Sudden assault using cold steel to maximize panic.

movements.label: Wayne's Withdrawal
movements.description: General Wayne attempts to organize a retreat amidst the chaos.

zones.label: American Camp Zone
zones.description: Area of the surprise attack.

zones.label: Battle Vicinity
zones.description: The surrounding wooded terrain of the skirmish.

battle of germantown.name: Battle of Germantown
battle of germantown.description: Washington's ambitious four-pronged attack on the British camp in Germantown failed due to thick fog and confusion, particularly a delay at the Chew House.
battle of germantown.significance: Showed American audacity despite the failure; impressed European observers with American resilience.

markers.label: Cliveden (Chew House)
markers.description: Stone mansion where 120 British soldiers held off American attacks.

markers.label: Sullivan's Advance
markers.description: Main American column attacking from the north.

markers.label: Friendly Fire Incident
markers.description: American units fire on each other in the dense fog.

markers.label: Germantown Market
markers.description: Center of the British camp.

markers.label: Greene's Column
markers.description: The delayed left wing finally joins the battle.

movements.label: Washington's Approach
movements.description: Complex night march to strike the British early morning.

movements.label: Retreat to Cliveden
movements.description: Musgrave's men take cover in the stone house as the front line breaks.

movements.label: General Retreat
movements.description: Panic sets in and the Americans withdraw in disorder.

zones.label: Cliveden Grounds
zones.description: Site of the protracted siege within the battle.

zones.label: Main British Camp
zones.description: The widespread deployment of Howe's army.

battle of saratoga.name: Battle of Saratoga
battle of saratoga.description: Two pivotal battles (Freeman's Farm and Bemis Heights) resulted in the defeat and surrender of General Burgoyne's army.
battle of saratoga.significance: The turning point of the war; this victory convinced France to enter as an official ally.

markers.label: Bemis Heights
markers.description: Fortified American positions overlooking the Hudson River road.

markers.label: Freeman's Farm
markers.description: Site of the first battle on September 19.

markers.label: Morgan's Riflemen
markers.description: Elite sharpshooters targeting British officers and artillerymen.

markers.label: Breymann Redoubt
markers.description: Hessian position stormed by Benedict Arnold during the second battle.

markers.label: Surrender Site
markers.description: Where Burgoyne surrendered his sword to Gates.

movements.label: British Invasion
movements.description: Burgoyne's army moves south to cut off New England.

movements.label: Arnold's Attack
movements.description: Defying orders, Arnold leads a decisive assault on the British right.

movements.label: Retreat to Schuylerville
movements.description: Broken British forces fall back to their final camp.

zones.label: Kosciuszko's Fortifications
zones.description: Impregnable works designed by Thaddeus Kosciuszko.

zones.label: Battleground North
zones.description: Area of heavy skirmishing and forest combat.

battle of monmouth.name: Battle of Monmouth
battle of monmouth.description: Fought in extreme heat, this battle saw the Continental Army demonstrate its new training under von Steuben, resulting in a standoff that ended the British retreat from Philadelphia.
battle of monmouth.significance: Proved the effectiveness of the training received at Valley Forge.

markers.label: Lee's Withdrawal
markers.description: General Lee orders a retreat that infuriates Washington.

markers.label: Molly Pitcher Well
markers.description: Legendary site where Mary Ludwig Hays aided the artillery.

markers.label: Washington's Stand
markers.description: Washington personally halts the retreat and reforms the line.

markers.label: Combs Hill
markers.description: American artillery position that swept the British flank.

markers.label: British Rear Guard
markers.description: British positions near the courthouse before the battle.

movements.label: Lee's Advance
movements.description: The American vanguard moves to strike the British baggage train.

movements.label: Cornwallis's Assault
movements.description: The British turn back to engage and push back Lee.

movements.label: Stealthy Withdrawal
movements.description: British forces slip away in the night to Sandy Hook.

zones.label: Pitched Battle Area
zones.description: Where the primary lines engaged for hours in the heat.

zones.label: Perrine's Hill Line
zones.description: Washington's defensive line after reforming.

battle of cowpens.name: Battle of Cowpens
battle of cowpens.description: A tactical masterpiece by Daniel Morgan, who used a 'double envelopment' to destroy Tarleton's force of British regulars.
battle of cowpens.significance: A major turning point in the southern campaign, depriving the British of their light infantry.

markers.label: Militia Skirmishers
markers.description: First line instructed to fire two shots and withdraw.

markers.label: Continental Line
markers.description: The solid center of Morgan's defense.

markers.label: Tarleton's Attack
markers.description: Aggressive but premature assault by British dragoons.

markers.label: William Washington's Cavalry
markers.description: American horsemen waiting to flank the British.

markers.label: British Collapse
markers.description: British infantry breaks and surrenders in large numbers.

movements.label: Feigned Retreat
movements.description: Militia withdraws as planned, drawing the British into a trap.

movements.label: The Flanking Move
movements.description: Pickens' militia and Washington's cavalry strike both British flanks.

movements.label: Tarleton Escapes
movements.description: Tarleton and a few dragoons flee the field.

zones.label: Hannah's Cowpens
zones.description: The open grazing land chosen by Morgan for the battle.

zones.label: American Reserve Zone
zones.description: Where troops reformed for the final counter-attack.

battle of guilford court house.name: Battle of Guilford Court House
battle of guilford court house.description: Greene used a defense-in-depth strategy against Cornwallis. While the British held the field, they lost a quarter of their army, leading to a strategic failure.
battle of guilford court house.significance: Severely weakened Cornwallis's army and forced him to abandon the Carolinas for Virginia.

markers.label: First Line (NC Militia)
markers.description: Positioned behind a rail fence to deliver the initial volleys.

markers.label: Second Line (VA Militia)
markers.description: Positioned in dense woods to harass the British advance.

markers.label: Third Line (Continentals)
markers.description: Greene's elite regulars near the Court House.

markers.label: Cornwallis Fires on Own Men
markers.description: British artillery fires into a melee to stop an American charge.

markers.label: Retreat to Speedwell
markers.description: Greene withdraws his army intact after inflicting heavy losses.

movements.label: Assault Through Woods
movements.description: British regulars struggle through thick brush against snipers.

movements.label: 1st Maryland Charge
movements.description: Elite Continentals counter-attack the charging British guards.

movements.label: Withdrawal to Wilmington
movements.description: Cornwallis abandons the field days later to seek supplies.

zones.label: Battlefield Forest
zones.description: Dense terrain that broke up British formations.

zones.label: Court House Area
zones.description: The final defensive position and supply hub.

siege of yorktown.name: Siege of Yorktown
siege of yorktown.description: The combined American and French forces trapped Cornwallis on a peninsula. After a prolonged siege and the capture of key redoubts, the British were forced to surrender.
siege of yorktown.significance: The last major land battle of the war, leading directly to the Treaty of Paris and American independence.

markers.label: Redoubt No. 9
markers.description: Stormed by French troops during the night of October 14.

markers.label: Redoubt No. 10
markers.description: Stormed by American troops led by Alexander Hamilton.

markers.label: Cornwallis's HQ
markers.description: The British commander's shelter during the heavy bombardment.

markers.label: Surrender Field
markers.description: Where the British army marched out to lay down their arms.

markers.label: First Parallel
markers.description: The first line of siege trenches dug by the allies.

movements.label: Closing the Trap
movements.description: American and French forces move to invest the town.

movements.label: French Fleet Blockade
movements.description: Admiral de Grasse prevents British escape or reinforcement by sea.

movements.label: Night Assault
movements.description: Bayonet attack to take out the remaining outer defenses.

zones.label: British Main Line
zones.description: The fortified perimeter around Yorktown village.

zones.label: French Sector
zones.description: The western half of the allied siege lines.

battle of queenston heights.name: Battle of Queenston Heights
battle of queenston heights.description: The first major land battle of the War of 1812, resulting from a failed American attempt to establish a foothold on the Canadian side of the Niagara River.
battle of queenston heights.significance: Resulted in the death of British General Isaac Brock but secured Upper Canada against early invasion.

markers.label: Vrooman's Point Battery
markers.description: British artillery position overlooking the river crossing.

markers.label: American Landing Site
markers.description: Where the first wave of American troops crossed from Lewiston.

markers.label: Brock's Fall
markers.description: General Brock is killed while leading a counter-attack on the heights.

markers.label: The V-Outpost
markers.description: Small British fortification taken early by American troops.

markers.label: Sheaffe's Arrival
markers.description: Reinforcements arrive from Fort George to flank the American position.

movements.label: American Crossing
movements.description: Troops cross the turbulent Niagara River under fire.

movements.label: Brock's Counter-attack
movements.description: Heroic but fatal uphill charge to retake the battery.

movements.label: Sheaffe's Maneuver
movements.description: British move around the heights to trap Americans against the cliff.

zones.label: The Heights
zones.description: The strategic high ground above the village.

battle of lake erie.name: Battle of Lake Erie
battle of lake erie.description: A major naval engagement where Oliver Hazard Perry's fleet defeated and captured a British squadron.
battle of lake erie.significance: Gave Americans control of Lake Erie for the rest of the war.

markers.label: USS Lawrence
markers.description: Perry's original flagship, heavily battered early in the fight.

markers.label: The Transfer
markers.description: Perry rows from the Lawrence to the Niagara mid-battle.

markers.label: HMS Detroit
markers.description: Barclay's flagship and the center of British resistance.

movements.label: Breaking the British Line
movements.description: Perry sails the Niagara through the British line, firing broadsides.

zones.label: Naval Engagement Zone
zones.description: The open water combat area.

battle of the thames.name: Battle of the Thames
battle of the thames.description: An American victory over British and Native American forces, following the retreat from Detroit.
battle of the thames.significance: Resulted in the death of Tecumseh and the destruction of the Native American coalition.

markers.label: Tecumseh's Fall
markers.description: The legendary Shawnee leader is killed during the engagement.

markers.label: Procter's Position
markers.description: British regular troops formed here before their quick collapse.

movements.label: Mounted Riflemen Charge
movements.description: Johnson's cavalry charges through the British lines.

zones.label: The Swampy Woods
zones.description: Where the Native American forces held their line.

battle of horseshoe bend.name: Battle of Horseshoe Bend
battle of horseshoe bend.description: Andrew Jackson's forces defeated the Red Stick Creeks, effectively ending the Creek War.
battle of horseshoe bend.significance: Cleared the way for American expansion in the Southeast.

markers.label: The Log Breastwork
markers.description: The formidable Red Stick defensive wall across the peninsula.

markers.label: Jackson's Battery
markers.description: Artillery used to batter the Creek fortifications.

movements.label: Cherokee Crossing
movements.description: Jackson's allies cross the river to attack the village from the rear.

zones.label: The Bend
zones.description: The trap-like peninsula of the Tallapoosa River.

battle of bladensburg.name: Battle of Bladensburg
battle of bladensburg.description: British forces routed a larger American force, clearing the way for the occupation of Washington D.C.
battle of bladensburg.significance: Often called the 'Bladensburg Races' due to the speed of the American retreat.

markers.label: Barney's Stand
markers.description: The only effective American resistance, led by sailors and marines.

markers.label: The Bridge
markers.description: Initial point of British crossing and contact.

movements.label: Congreve Rockets
movements.description: British use of rockets that caused panic in American militia.

zones.label: Path of Retreat
zones.description: The disorganized withdrawal toward the capital.

burning of washington.name: Burning of Washington
burning of washington.description: British troops occupied the American capital and set fire to numerous public buildings, including the White House and the Capitol.
burning of washington.significance: The only time since the Revolution that a foreign power has captured the U.S. capital.

markers.label: The White House
markers.description: The presidential mansion burned by British troops.

markers.label: The Capitol
markers.description: The seat of the U.S. legislature set ablaze.

markers.label: Washington Navy Yard
markers.description: Burned by Americans to prevent capture of ships and stores.

movements.label: British Entry
movements.description: Troops enter the city following the victory at Bladensburg.

zones.label: Captured City
zones.description: The area of Washington occupied by the British.

battle of baltimore.name: Battle of Baltimore
battle of baltimore.description: A combined sea and land battle. The successful defense of Fort McHenry inspired the Star-Spangled Banner.
battle of baltimore.significance: Halted the British offensive in the Chesapeake and restored American morale.

markers.label: Fort McHenry
markers.description: The star-shaped fort that withstood 25 hours of bombardment.

markers.label: Battle of North Point
markers.description: The land engagement where General Ross was killed.

markers.label: Ross's Fall
markers.description: The British commander is shot by American snipers.

movements.label: Naval Bombardment
movements.description: British ships launch rockets and shells at the fort.

zones.label: Rodgers' Bastion
zones.description: Extensive earthworks that deterred a British land assault.

battle of plattsburgh.name: Battle of Plattsburgh
battle of plattsburgh.description: An American naval victory on Lake Champlain that forced a large British army to retreat to Canada.
battle of plattsburgh.significance: Ended the final British invasion of the northern states.

markers.label: USS Saratoga
markers.description: Macdonough used kedge anchors to wind his ship and present a fresh broadside.

markers.label: Fort Moreau
markers.description: One of the key American redoubts on the land side.

movements.label: Downie's Approach
movements.description: The British squadron enters the bay to engage the anchored Americans.

zones.label: Plattsburgh Bay
zones.description: The confined waters of the naval engagement.

battle of new orleans.name: Battle of New Orleans
battle of new orleans.description: The final major battle of the war, occurring after the peace treaty was signed but before news reached the U.S.
battle of new orleans.significance: Propelled Andrew Jackson to national fame and cemented American sovereignty.

markers.label: Line Jackson
markers.description: The heavily fortified American line behind a canal.

markers.label: Pakenham's Death
markers.description: The British commander is mortally wounded while trying to rally his troops.

markers.label: West Bank Position
markers.description: American guns on the opposite bank that raked the British flank.

movements.label: The Main Charge
movements.description: British infantry advances through fog into devastating fire.

zones.label: The Plain of Chalmette
zones.description: The narrow strip of land between the river and the swamp.

battle of fort sumter.name: Battle of Fort Sumter
battle of fort sumter.description: The opening engagement of the American Civil War. Confederate batteries fired on the Federal fort in Charleston Harbor, leading to its surrender.
battle of fort sumter.significance: Marked the beginning of open hostilities and the start of the Civil War.

markers.label: Fort Sumter
markers.description: The Federal garrison under Major Anderson.

markers.label: Fort Johnson
markers.description: Confederate battery that fired the opening shot.

first battle of bull run.name: First Battle of Bull Run
first battle of bull run.description: The first major land battle of the war. Union forces initially pushed back the Confederates, but a rally on Henry House Hill led to a Union rout.
first battle of bull run.significance: Shattered the Northern hope for a short, 90-day war.

markers.label: Henry House Hill
markers.description: The decisive high ground and site of Jackson's stand.

battle of shiloh.name: Battle of Shiloh
battle of shiloh.description: A massive surprise attack by Confederates on Grant's camp. The arrival of Union reinforcements on the second day turned the tide.
battle of shiloh.significance: The bloodiest battle in U.S. history up to that point; shocked both North and South.

markers.label: Shiloh Church
markers.description: The center of the initial Confederate assault.

markers.label: The Hornet's Nest
markers.description: Union troops held this sunken road for hours against repeated assaults.

markers.label: Pittsburg Landing
markers.description: Union supply point and arrival site of reinforcements.

movements.label: Johnston's Attack
movements.description: Initial surprise push on the morning of April 6.

battle of antietam.name: Battle of Antietam
battle of antietam.description: The bloodiest single day in American history. McClellan intercepted Lee's invasion of Maryland, leading to a tactical standoff.
battle of antietam.significance: Gave Lincoln the political capital to issue the Emancipation Proclamation.

markers.label: The Cornfield
markers.description: Site of some of the war's most intense infantry combat.

markers.label: Sunken Road (Bloody Lane)
markers.description: Confederate center held this position until overwhelmed.

markers.label: Burnside's Bridge
markers.description: Union troops fought for hours to cross this bridge.

battle of fredericksburg.name: Battle of Fredericksburg
battle of fredericksburg.description: A disastrous Union assault on Lee's fortified positions behind the town and on Marye's Heights.
battle of fredericksburg.significance: One of the most lopsided Confederate victories of the war.

markers.label: Marye's Heights
markers.description: Confederate infantry behind a stone wall decimated Union charges.

battle of chancellorsville.name: Battle of Chancellorsville
battle of chancellorsville.description: Lee's 'perfect battle', where he divided his outnumbered army to flank and defeat Hooker's Union force.
battle of chancellorsville.significance: Resulted in the death of 'Stonewall' Jackson due to friendly fire.

markers.label: Jackson's Flank Attack
markers.description: Site where Jackson's corps struck the Union 11th Corps.

movements.label: Jackson's Night March
movements.description: Silent flanking maneuver to get behind Union lines.

battle of gettysburg.name: Battle of Gettysburg
battle of gettysburg.description: The turning point of the war. Lee's second invasion of the North was halted by the Army of the Potomac.
battle of gettysburg.significance: Ended Confederate hopes for recognition and Lee's offensive capacity.

markers.label: Little Round Top
markers.description: Vital anchor of the Union left flank.

siege of vicksburg.name: Siege of Vicksburg
siege of vicksburg.description: Union forces under Grant surrounded and starved the city until it surrendered on July 4.
siege of vicksburg.significance: Gave the Union total control of the Mississippi River.

battle of chickamauga.name: Battle of Chickamauga
battle of chickamauga.description: A massive Confederate counter-attack that forced the Union army to retreat to Chattanooga.
battle of chickamauga.significance: The largest Union defeat in the Western Theater.

markers.label: Snodgrass Hill
markers.description: George Thomas's stand that earned him the name 'Rock of Chickamauga'.

battle of the wilderness.name: Battle of the Wilderness
battle of the wilderness.description: The first meeting of Grant and Lee. Chaos in dense woods where the undergrowth caught fire.
battle of the wilderness.significance: Grant refused to retreat, unlike his predecessors, and moved south.

markers.label: Saunders Field
markers.description: Open ground in the woods where intense fighting occurred.

battle of spotsylvania court house.name: Battle of Spotsylvania Court House
battle of spotsylvania court house.description: Continuation of Grant's Overland Campaign, featuring the brutal hand-to-hand combat at the 'Bloody Angle'.
battle of spotsylvania court house.significance: Demonstrated the new reality of continuous, grinding warfare.

markers.label: The Bloody Angle
markers.description: A salient where troops fought for 22 hours straight.

battle of cold harbor.name: Battle of Cold Harbor
battle of cold harbor.description: A bloody frontal assault by Grant's forces against Lee's entrenched positions.
battle of cold harbor.significance: One of Grant's greatest regrets; massive Union losses in minutes.

markers.label: Lee's Earthworks
markers.description: Formidable trench systems that rendered Union attacks suicidal.

battle of atlanta.name: Battle of Atlanta
battle of atlanta.description: Sherman defeated Hood outside the city, leading to the eventual fall of the strategic Southern rail hub.
battle of atlanta.significance: The fall of Atlanta ensured Lincoln's re-election in 1864.

markers.label: Bald Hill
markers.description: Key high ground captured by Union troops during the battle.

battle of franklin.name: Battle of Franklin
battle of franklin.description: Hood launched a massive, futile frontal assault on Union fortifications, devastating his own army.
battle of franklin.significance: Resulted in the death of six Confederate generals.

markers.label: Carter House
markers.description: Center of the Union defensive line.

battle of nashville.name: Battle of Nashville
battle of nashville.description: Thomas effectively destroyed Hood's Army of Tennessee in a massive two-day assault.
battle of nashville.significance: Ended the major fighting in the Western Theater.

markers.label: Shy's Hill
markers.description: Site of the final Confederate collapse on Day 2.

battle of appomattox court house.name: Battle of Appomattox Court House
battle of appomattox court house.description: Lee's final attempt to break through Union lines; his subsequent surrender ended the war in Virginia.
battle of appomattox court house.significance: Effectively signaled the end of the American Civil War.

markers.label: McLean House
markers.description: Where Lee signed the surrender document.

battle of trafalgar.name: Battle of Trafalgar
battle of trafalgar.description: The most decisive naval battle of the Napoleonic Wars. Nelson's British fleet destroyed the combined French and Spanish fleet, ensuring British naval supremacy for a century.
battle of trafalgar.significance: Ended Napoleon's plans for an invasion of Britain and established the Royal Navy as the world's dominant sea power.

markers.label: HMS Victory
markers.description: Nelson's flagship at the head of the weather column.

markers.label: HMS Royal Sovereign
markers.description: Collingwood's flagship, the first to break the enemy line.

markers.label: Bucentaure
markers.description: Villeneuve's flagship, located in the center of the allied crescent.

markers.label: Nelson Struck
markers.description: Admiral Nelson is mortally wounded by a French marksman.

markers.label: Santissima Trinidad
markers.description: The massive Spanish four-decker ship of the line.

movements.label: Nelson's Column
movements.description: The northern British column attacking the allied van and center.

movements.label: Collingwood's Column
movements.description: The southern column aimed at the allied rear.

movements.label: Allied Van Maneuver
movements.description: Admiral Dumanoir's attempt to turn and assist the center.

zones.label: Main Engagement Zone
zones.description: The area where the two lines clashed in a chaotic 'pelle-melle' fight.

battle of austerlitz.name: Battle of Austerlitz
battle of austerlitz.description: Napoleon's greatest victory, also known as the Battle of the Three Emperors. He lured the Russo-Austrian forces into a trap and shattered their center on the Pratzen Heights.
battle of austerlitz.significance: Led to the collapse of the Third Coalition and the Treaty of Pressburg.

markers.label: Pratzen Heights
markers.description: The strategic center of the battlefield abandoned by the Allies.

markers.label: Soult's Masterstroke
markers.description: The IV Corps storms the heights to split the Allied army.

markers.label: Davout at Telnitz
markers.description: Davout's exhausted corps holds the French right against heavy odds.

markers.label: Allied Headquarters
markers.description: Command post for the Emperors Alexander and Francis.

markers.label: Satschan Ponds
markers.description: Site where retreating Russians supposedly drowned through broken ice.

movements.label: Assault on the Heights
movements.description: The decisive upward movement of St. Hilaire and Vandamme's divisions.

movements.label: Allied Left Attack
movements.description: Buxhowden's massive columns move to crush the French right.

movements.label: Guard Engagement
movements.description: Clash between the French and Russian Imperial Guards.

zones.label: Pratzen Plateau
zones.description: The high ground that decided the fate of the battle.

battle of waterloo.name: Battle of Waterloo
battle of waterloo.description: The final defeat of Napoleon Bonaparte. A Coalition army under Wellington held their ground until Blucher's Prussians arrived to crush the French flank.
battle of waterloo.significance: Marked the end of the Napoleonic Wars and the final exile of Napoleon.

markers.label: Hougoumont
markers.description: Stronghold on the Allied right that distracted the French all day.

markers.label: La Haye Sainte
markers.description: Farmhouse in the center of the line, eventually captured by Ney.

markers.label: Wellington's Command
markers.description: The Duke's position behind the ridge.

markers.label: Plancenoit
markers.description: Where the Prussians engaged the French Young Guard.

markers.label: The Old Guard
markers.description: Final stand of the Imperial Guard as the army flees.

movements.label: D'Erlon's Assault
movements.description: Massive French infantry column attack on the Allied left.

movements.label: Ney's Cavalry Charges
movements.description: Massive, unsupported French cavalry assaults on British squares.

movements.label: Blucher's Advance
movements.description: The timely arrival of Prussian IV Corps.

zones.label: Allied Defensive Line
zones.description: The ridge used by Wellington to hide his troops from artillery.

battle of jena.name: Battle of Jena
battle of jena.description: Simultaneous battles at Jena and Auerstedt led to the total destruction of the Prussian army by Napoleon's forces.
battle of jena.significance: Knocked Prussia out of the war and led to the French occupation of Berlin.

markers.label: Landgrafenberg
markers.description: Steep heights scaled by French troops at night.

markers.label: Vierzehnheiligen
markers.description: Village at the center of the heaviest fighting.

movements.label: Napoleonic Push
movements.description: French center and right sweep the Prussians from the field.

zones.label: Jena Battlefield
zones.description: The broken terrain where Napoleon trapped the Prussians.

battle of borodino.name: Battle of Borodino
battle of borodino.description: The bloodiest single day of the Napoleonic Wars. A massive frontal engagement that allowed Napoleon to enter Moscow, but failed to destroy the Russian army.
battle of borodino.significance: Weakened the Grande Armée fatally before the retreat from Moscow.

markers.label: Shevardino Redoubt
markers.description: Outpost captured by the French two days before the main battle.

markers.label: Bagration Fleches
markers.description: V-shaped earthworks that saw horrific slaughter.

markers.label: The Great Redoubt
markers.description: The center of the Russian line, eventually taken by French cavalry.

movements.label: Attack on Fleches
movements.description: Davout's corps repeatedly assaults the Russian left.

zones.label: The Killing Fields
zones.description: Area of dense artillery fire and infantry massacres.

battle of leipzig.name: Battle of Leipzig
battle of leipzig.description: The 'Battle of the Nations'. The largest battle in Europe prior to WWI, involving over 600,000 soldiers.
battle of leipzig.significance: Destroyed Napoleon's remaining power in Germany and Poland.

markers.label: Elster Bridge
markers.description: Prematurely blown up, trapping thousands of French troops in the city.

markers.label: Probstheida
markers.description: The village that formed the anchor of the French defense.

movements.label: Converging Columns
movements.description: Allied armies attack from all directions to surround Napoleon.

zones.label: Battle of Nations Area
zones.description: Massive multi-sector battlefield surrounding the city.

battle of the marne.name: Battle of the Marne
battle of the marne.description: A strategic Allied victory that halted the German advance into France and ended German hopes for a quick victory in the West.
battle of the marne.significance: Marked the failure of the Schlieffen Plan and the beginning of trench warfare.

markers.label: Taxi Embarkation
markers.description: 600 Paris taxis carry reinforcements to the front.

markers.label: The Gap
markers.description: A 30-mile gap opens between the German 1st and 2nd Armies.

markers.label: French General HQ
markers.description: Joffre coordinates the 'Miracle on the Marne' counter-offensive.

movements.label: Von Kluck's Turn
movements.description: German 1st Army turns east of Paris, exposing its flank.

movements.label: Attack on the Ourcq
movements.description: French 6th Army strikes the exposed German flank.

zones.label: The Marne Front
zones.description: The general line of engagement across the river basin.

battle of tannenberg.name: Battle of Tannenberg
battle of tannenberg.description: A crushing German victory over the Russian Second Army, resulting in its near-total destruction.
battle of tannenberg.significance: Proved that Germany could win on the Eastern Front despite being outnumbered.

markers.label: Samsonov's Suicide
markers.description: The Russian General takes his life after the total defeat.

markers.label: German Field HQ
markers.description: Commanders execute the encirclement of the Russian forces.

movements.label: Southern Pincer
movements.description: German XX Corps blocks the Russian southern escape.

zones.label: The Encirclement
zones.description: Russian 2nd Army is surrounded in the swampy woods.

battle of gallipoli.name: Battle of Gallipoli
battle of gallipoli.description: An ambitious Allied naval and land campaign to seize the Dardanelles straits that ended in a costly stalemate and withdrawal.
battle of gallipoli.significance: A defining moment for Turkish national identity and the ANZAC legacy.

markers.label: Anzac Cove
markers.description: Site of the Australian and New Zealand landings.

markers.label: V Beach
markers.description: Site of the landing from the SS River Clyde at Cape Helles.

markers.label: Chunuk Bair
markers.description: Kemal tells his men: 'I do not order you to fight, I order you to die.'

movements.label: Cape Helles Landing
movements.description: British troops land at the southern tip of the peninsula.

zones.label: Suvla Bay
zones.description: August landings aimed at breaking the stalemate.

battle of verdun.name: Battle of Verdun
battle of verdun.description: The longest battle of WWI, intended by Falkenhayn to 'bleed France white' within a narrow salient.
battle of verdun.significance: A symbol of French determination and a catastrophe of industrial attrition.

markers.label: Fort Douaumont
markers.description: Key fortification captured easily by Germans, then retaken by French.

markers.label: Voie Sacrée
markers.description: The 'Sacred Way' supply road that kept Verdun alive.

markers.label: Thiaumont Farm
markers.description: Site of devastating close-quarters combat.

movements.label: Opening Salvo
movements.description: Massive 1,200-gun bombardment starts the battle.

zones.label: The Verdun Sector
zones.description: Area of the concentrated German offensive.

battle of the somme.name: Battle of the Somme
battle of the somme.description: A joint British-French offensive designed to break the German lines and relieve pressure on Verdun.
battle of the somme.significance: Infamous for the heaviest one-day loss in British military history.

markers.label: Thiepval Ridge
markers.description: Strongly fortified German position held for months.

markers.label: Lochnagar Mine
markers.description: Largest mine detonated on the first day of the battle.

markers.label: Flers-Courcelette
markers.description: First use of tanks in warfare history.

movements.label: July 1st Advance
movements.description: Wave after wave of British troops go 'over the top'.

zones.label: No Man's Land
zones.description: The desolate space between the main trench systems.

battle of passchendaele.name: Battle of Passchendaele
battle of passchendaele.description: Also known as the Third Battle of Ypres, remembered for the horrific mud and rain that hindered operations.
battle of passchendaele.significance: A grueling war of attrition that came to symbolize the horrors of the Western Front.

markers.label: Ypres
markers.description: The ruined city serving as the Allied springboard.

markers.label: Passchendaele Ruins
markers.description: The village captured by Canadians at the end of the battle.

movements.label: The Slough of Despond
movements.description: Infantry struggling through waist-deep mud and shell holes.

zones.label: The Salient
zones.description: The protruding Allied line surrounded by German heights.

battle of cambrai.name: Battle of Cambrai
battle of cambrai.description: The first large-scale and successful use of tanks in a combined arms offensive.
battle of cambrai.significance: Revolutionized modern warfare by demonstrating the power of armored formations.

markers.label: Bourlon Wood
markers.description: Key high ground that saw intense fighting during the advance.

markers.label: Line Breach
markers.description: Tanks easily crush the barbed wire of the Hindenburg Line.

movements.label: Iron Cavalry
movements.description: 476 tanks advance without a preliminary bombardment.

movements.label: Stormtroop Attack
movements.description: German infiltration tactics retake much of the lost ground.

zones.label: The Cambrai Sector
zones.description: Target area of the armored breakthrough.

battle of britain.name: Battle of Britain
battle of britain.description: The first major military campaign fought entirely by air forces. The RAF successfully defended the UK against large-scale attacks by the Luftwaffe.
battle of britain.significance: Prevented Operation Sea Lion, the planned German invasion of Britain.

markers.label: Fighter Command HQ
markers.description: Command center for the Dowding System of air defense.

markers.label: Chain Home Radar
markers.description: Key early warning radar station detecting incoming raids.

markers.label: RAF Biggin Hill
markers.description: Critical sector station targeted heavily during the battle.

markers.label: Target: London
markers.description: German shift to bombing cities to break civilian morale.

movements.label: Luftflotte 2 Strike
movements.description: German bomber formations crossing the Channel from occupied France.

movements.label: RAF Interception
movements.description: Spitfires and Hurricanes scrambled to engage bombers.

zones.label: 11 Group Airspace
zones.description: The most active sector covering Southeast England.

battle of stalingrad.name: Battle of Stalingrad
battle of stalingrad.description: One of the bloodiest battles in history, involving brutal urban warfare and a massive Soviet encirclement of the German 6th Army.
battle of stalingrad.significance: The turning point of the war on the Eastern Front.

markers.label: Mamayev Kurgan
markers.description: Strategic hill overlooking the city, contested constantly.

markers.label: Pavlov's House
markers.description: Fortified apartment building held by a small Soviet platoon.

markers.label: Grain Elevator
markers.description: Scene of ferocious room-to-room fighting.

markers.label: Univermag Basement
markers.description: Paulus surrenders his remaining forces to the Soviets.

movements.label: Operation Uranus
movements.description: Soviet pincer movement to encircle the German 6th Army.

zones.label: The Kessel
zones.description: The area where German forces were trapped without supplies.

battle of midway.name: Battle of Midway
battle of midway.description: A decisive naval battle in the Pacific Theater where the US Navy sank four Japanese aircraft carriers.
battle of midway.significance: Shattered Japanese naval superiority and halted Japanese expansion.

markers.label: USS Yorktown
markers.description: American carrier severely damaged and eventually sunk.

markers.label: IJN Kaga
markers.description: One of the four Japanese carriers destroyed in the morning strike.

movements.label: SBD Dauntless Attack
movements.description: US dive bombers find the Japanese carriers at their most vulnerable.

zones.label: Midway Atoll
zones.description: Strategic target of the Japanese invasion plan.

d-day — normandy landings.name: D-Day — Normandy Landings
d-day — normandy landings.description: The largest seaborne invasion in history, initiating the Allied effort to liberate Western Europe from Nazi control.
d-day — normandy landings.significance: Opened the Second Front in Europe and forced Germany to fight on two fronts.

markers.label: Omaha Beach
markers.description: The most heavily defended landing sector for US troops.

markers.label: Pegasus Bridge
markers.description: Captured by British gliders in the early hours.

markers.label: Pointe du Hoc
markers.description: US Rangers scaled these cliffs to disable German guns.

markers.label: Sainte-Mère-Église
markers.description: First town liberated by US paratroopers.

movements.label: Operation Neptune
movements.description: Allied armada crossing the English Channel.

zones.label: Atlantic Wall
zones.description: System of coastal fortifications built by Germany.

battle of the bulge.name: Battle of the Bulge
battle of the bulge.description: The last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front, creating a 'bulge' in the Allied lines.
battle of the bulge.significance: Exhausted Germany's armored reserves and accelerated the end of the war.

markers.label: Bastogne
markers.description: The 101st Airborne held this crossroads despite being surrounded.

markers.label: Malmedy
markers.description: Site of the execution of American POWs by the SS.

movements.label: Wacht am Rhein
movements.description: The surprise German blitzkrieg through the snowy Ardennes.

movements.label: Patton's Relief
movements.description: The 3rd Army's rapid northward turn to relieve Bastogne.

zones.label: The Salient
zones.description: The maximum extent of the German breakthrough.

battle of iwo jima.name: Battle of Iwo Jima
battle of iwo jima.description: A major battle in which the US Marine Corps landed on and eventually captured the island of Iwo Jima from the Imperial Japanese Army.
battle of iwo jima.significance: Provided a strategic landing site for US bombers striking Japan.

markers.label: Mount Suribachi
markers.description: The highest point on the island and site of the famous flag raising.

markers.label: Airfield No. 1
markers.description: A primary objective of the invasion.

movements.label: Beach Landings
movements.description: Marines struggle through volcanic ash under intense fire.

zones.label: Cave Defense System
zones.description: Extensive network of tunnels used by Japanese defenders.

battle of berlin.name: Battle of Berlin
battle of berlin.description: The final major offensive of the European theater, where Soviet forces captured the capital of Nazi Germany.
battle of berlin.significance: Led to the unconditional surrender of Germany and the end of Hitler's regime.

markers.label: The Reichstag
markers.description: Target of intense symbolic fighting and site of the Soviet flag raising.

markers.label: Führerbunker
markers.description: The underground shelter where Hitler spent his final days.

markers.label: Seelow Heights
markers.description: The 'Gates to Berlin' where Soviets broke the final defensive line.

movements.label: The Trap Closes
movements.description: Zhukov and Konev's fronts converge to isolate the city.

zones.label: The Citadel
zones.description: The inner defensive sector of government buildings.

operation market garden.name: Operation Market Garden
operation market garden.description: An unsuccessful Allied military operation to create a salient into Germany using airborne and armored forces.
operation market garden.significance: One of the most significant Allied failures of the war.

markers.label: A Bridge Too Far
markers.description: The final bridge at Arnhem that the Allies failed to secure.

markers.label: Nijmegen Bridge
markers.description: Captured in a heroic river crossing by US paratroopers.

movements.label: XXX Corps Advance
movements.description: The armored column attempting to link up with paratroopers.

zones.label: Paratrooper Drop Zones
zones.description: Where British airborne forces landed far from their objective.
